## [00:00 - 03:01]

Now we're going to make an actual ad campaign and to do that you head to business.facebook.com if you're not already there and you may or may not have access to more than one account here but you want to choose the proper account on the left hand side and then as far as ad accounts you might have more than one but if you follow the setup so far you should just have the one so we'll just choose that. And that will open up in the ads manager like this. You simply hit create over here on the top left and when this comes up generally we only use two different kinds either lead generation or conversions and it's almost always conversions so we'll just choose that for this campaign. Now you can name it at this step you don't have to what I usually do is I just have some sort of placeholder so I'll just type something in here to get us going. Doesn't matter what you put here it's just a placeholder like I said hit continue and then when it's up and running like this it's open in this window and you can see the different campaigns down the left here you should just have the one and in this middle bracket you can see the different levels so there's campaign level, ad set level and ad level. Now you can work in either one of these views what I usually do is close this and then I can see all my ad sets all together sorry all my campaigns together and I just click on the one I want either you click on the name or you can select it here and choose edit. Then that takes you into the ad set level if you click on that it takes you into the ad level so you can navigate this through these little tabs at the top so that just brought us back to campaign level and then if we go to edit route I'll show you both of these so you can see how they work. If we're in the campaign level here and you select the campaign you're interested in and choose edit that reopens that window so there's pretty much no way to get lost in this. Now with this open here your campaign name should say you'll probably come up with your own sort of naming convention but I like to list what I'm offering here what my offer is that's related to this campaign so it might be let's say RAI webinar 3 and then like I said I have my own convention you'll have your own and you can see here the one that popped up I state the thing that I'm offering what I want out of it I want registrations and then the type of campaign that it is you don't have to follow this exactly but we'll just do it like this for now registrations. There we go and then you don't have to do special ad categories unless you're in the

## [06:06 - 09:11]

versions of images video headlines and copy and have meta actually spread the budget amongst those for the most performance if you want to manually control it you would leave this off we're going to leave it off just for this example okay and then budget this is a very popular question what should my budget be set at if you think about your product let's say it's a $10 product and you want to get X many conversions per day your budget should be a multiple of that so if you if your product let's say is $10 and your budget is $10 per day you're probably not going to get a whole lot of conversions because it's not enough budget to get that many sales if it's $3,000 to your item you're going to have to have a lot bigger budget so one rule of thumb you can use is like three times the cost of the product so we could say $30 for our $10 product and another spot you might change this if you see this little drop down there's daily budget and there's also lifetime budget I generally start with daily budget and I would use lifetime budget if for example this campaign was going to run for a set amount of time and then I wanted it to shut off so if it's a time limited sort of thing maybe it's going to end on two weeks or September 1st or something like that you know a time out from whatever today is then I would choose that time and change my daily or my budget to to lifetime like I said there's limitations to that if you have a daily budget you go in here and if things are going really well you can change this and add budget to it so I generally keep away from the lifetime budget unless it's absolutely necessary you can always go in and turn your budget up or down or turn the ad sets on or off and that takes care of that section down here to audience so you're
